top_level_reasoning: All 5 fixes (FIX-A through FIX-E) substantively resolved. Master Canon label drift cleanly separated. Memory Spec §6.2 elegantly resolved with two-tier model. Lane Federation §6.1 vendor names removed. Capability Matrix §7 promoted as canonical side-effect spine with shared terminology. Backend/Event §21 mapping table aligns with canonical spine. No new authority drift, canon drift, schema drift, or taxonomy drift introduced. Ready for NTS decision/apply phase. Two minor non-blocking notes on Backend/Event candidate (§13.3 Capability Gate Outcomes still uses old 4-option set instead of matching §15.2's new ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY; §10.2 Event Contract field name 'risk_class' differs from §15.2 Action Contract 'backend_event_risk_class') — neither blocks NTS decision.

aier_code_master_canon_v_1_v0_2_candidat.verification: Header has Document ID + Document Family + Candidate Revision (v0.2) separated. Status CANDIDATE_REVISION/PENDING_REVIEW. Approval Status NOT APPROVED. Activation Status NOT ACTIVE. Revision Notes v0.2 explicitly state 'This candidate revision does not activate AIER_CODE_MASTER_CANON_V1'. Final line: DRAFT CANDIDATE — NOT canon. Clean fix.

aier_code_memory_spec_v_1_v0_2_candidate.verification: §6.2 Two-Tier Current State Schema explicit. §6.3 Tier A (current V0 operational schema) lists shipped 9-section structure (10 with CHANGELOG); explicit 'Do not harden runtime/current_state.md inside this candidate revision task'. §6.4 Tier B (future formal schema target) preserves the original 15 sections; explicit 'Recommended future task: T-RUNTIME-002'. Day-1 conformance failure cleanly resolved. shipped current_state.md preserved as ground truth until scoped state migration task. Excellent two-tier design.

aier_code_lane_federation_spec_v_1_v0_2_.verification: §6.1 Lane_03 role: 'execution + canon-boundary support' (vendor-neutral). Note below table: 'Tool/vendor/executor details may be recorded only in Lane infrastructure files, scoped reports, or operational notes, and must not define canon Lane identity.' §7 EXECUTION role updated to 'scoped execution under task authority'. §4.2 forbidden vendor names list preserved (Codex still listed as forbidden canon Lane identity). §4.3 Lane Identity Rule preserved. Internal contradiction resolved.

aier_code_capability_matrix_spec_v_1_v0_.verification: §6 Permission Classes preserved as permission_class only with explicit note 'Permission class is not the same as risk class or side-effect class.' §7 Side-Effect Classes (8 classes 0-7: NO_EFFECT/EVIDENCE_ONLY/DRAFT_CHANGE/ACTIVE_REPO_CHANGE/GOVERNANCE_CHANGE/EXTERNAL_SYSTEM_CHANGE/AUTONOMOUS_OPERATION/CRITICAL_CONTROL) promoted as canonical side-effect spine — exactly matches the spine I prescribed in FIX-D. §7 explicit: 'Side-effect classes 4-7 require NTS approval unless explicitly approved otherwise through lawful governance.' §7.1 Shared Terminology table defines 9 terms (permission_class/side_effect_class/risk_class/authority_gate/capability_gate/action_class/backend_event_action/external_effect/autonomous_operation). §7.2 Capability Gate Outcomes (ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY). §7.3 Cross-File Mapping Note: 'Productization UI action classes and draft LAW_N10 classes must later map to this canonical side-effect spine. This candidate revision does not edit Productization or SHARED/laws.' Excellent unification.

aier_code_backend_event_spec_v_1_v0_2_ca.verification: §15.2 Action Contract includes both side_effect_class (primary, integer 0-7) and backend_event_risk_class (derived enum LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL) — exactly what FIX-E required. §15.2 capability_check enum updated to ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY (matches Capability Matrix §7.2). §21 retitled 'Backend/Event Risk Classes' with explicit 'Backend/Event must not maintain a competing standalone impact taxonomy.' §21 mapping table LOW (0-1) / MEDIUM (2-3) / HIGH (4-5) / CRITICAL (6-7) — exactly the mapping I requested. §21 closing: 'Action contracts must include side_effect_class as the primary impact classification. backend_event_risk_class must be derived from side_effect_class, not chosen as an independent authority model.' Core FIX-E intent perfectly implemented.
non_blocking_notes: §13.3 Capability Gate Outcomes still uses old 4-option set (ALLOW/ALLOW_WITH_APPROVAL/BLOCK/ESCALATE) instead of matching Capability Matrix §7.2 canonical (ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY). §15.2 Action Contract uses the new set. Internal inconsistency between §13.3 and §15.2 should be reconciled — but doesn't change semantic intent (ALLOW_WITH_APPROVAL is roughly equivalent to NTS_ONLY).

§10.2 Event Contract field uses 'risk_class' (no prefix) while §15.2 Action Contract uses 'backend_event_risk_class' (with prefix). Cosmetic naming inconsistency — should be reconciled to single field name (recommend backend_event_risk_class everywhere).

fix_verification_matrix.FIX-B_memory_current_state_schema: PASS — two-tier model (Tier A shipped 9-section schema; Tier B future formal 15-section target deferred to T-RUNTIME-002); shipped file not modified; day-1 conformance preserved
fix_verification_matrix.FIX-C_lane_federation_vendor_identity: PASS — Lane_03 role vendor-neutral; vendor/tool details restricted to Lane infrastructure files; §4.2 forbidden vendor names preserved
fix_verification_matrix.FIX-D_capability_taxonomy_unification: PASS — Capability Matrix §7 8-class spine declared canonical; permission class kept separate; side-effect classes 4-7 NTS-gated; §7.1 Shared Terminology defines all 9 terms; §7.2 Capability Gate Outcomes (ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY)
fix_verification_matrix.FIX-E_backend_risk_mapping_terminology: PASS_WITH_NOTES — §21 mapping table LOW(0-1)/MEDIUM(2-3)/HIGH(4-5)/CRITICAL(6-7) clean; §15.2 action contract has both side_effect_class (primary) and backend_event_risk_class (derived); core mapping correct. Notes: §13.3 capability gate outcomes still 4-option old set; §10.2 event contract field name differs from §15.2 action contract field name.

answers_to_4_review_questions.Q1_5_fixes_implemented_correctly: YES — all 5 fixes (FIX-A through FIX-E) substantively resolved. FIX-E has 2 minor non-blocking terminology inconsistencies in Backend/Event candidate but core FIX-E intent (mapping table + side_effect_class as primary) is correctly implemented.
answers_to_4_review_questions.Q2_remaining_drift: NO new canon drift, authority drift, schema drift, or taxonomy drift introduced by the v0.2 candidates. The 2 minor non-blocking inconsistencies in Backend/Event candidate are within-file inconsistencies, not cross-file drift. Pre-existing path drift (D:\UZG\... in headers) and pre-existing source-draft Master Canon §13 vendor name (out of scope of FIX-C) are residual issues outside the scope of these 5 fixes — Lane_01 prior RSP flagged them as separate amendments needed.
answers_to_4_review_questions.Q3_clean_for_NTS: YES — candidates are clean enough to move to NTS decision/apply phase. The 2 non-blocking notes can be polished during apply task or deferred to v0.3.
answers_to_4_review_questions.Q4_another_round_required: NO — another amendment round is not required. NTS may proceed to decision/apply. If NTS prefers polish, the 2 non-blocking notes can be addressed in the apply task itself or in a small follow-up before apply.

remaining_blockers: None at the FIX-A-E level. All 5 fixes substantively resolved.
non_blocking_notes: Backend/Event §13.3 Capability Gate Outcomes still uses old 4-option set (ALLOW/ALLOW_WITH_APPROVAL/BLOCK/ESCALATE) — should be reconciled to Capability Matrix §7.2 canonical (ALLOW/BLOCK/ESCALATE/NTS_ONLY) for internal consistency. Doesn't block NTS decision.

Backend/Event §10.2 Event Contract field 'risk_class' vs §15.2 Action Contract field 'backend_event_risk_class' — naming inconsistency. Recommend backend_event_risk_class everywhere. Doesn't block NTS decision.

Master Canon §13 Lane Model still has 'AITAO + Codex / execution + canon-boundary support' for Lane_03 — same vendor name issue as FIX-C scope but in Master Canon (out of scope for FIX-C which only covered Lane Federation). Lane_03 may want to align Master Canon §13 with Lane Federation §6.1 for consistency. Pre-existing issue carried over from source draft; not introduced by v0.2.

Master Canon §14 still hard-codes D:\UZG\Projects-v2\Uniton_Shared as canonical repo path — same issue as ACTIVE WORKSPACE_REPO_INTEGRITY_POLICY.md flagged in prior CTO review (LANE01-CTO-OS-OPERATIONS-REVIEW-AND-UPDATE-V1). Pre-existing; not introduced by v0.2; needs Lane-aware refactor in separate amendment.

All 5 candidates carry 'Revision Author: Lane_03 / Codex' in metadata header — uses 'Codex' name in metadata. Per Lane Federation §4.2 vendor names are forbidden in canon Lane identity but permitted in implementation/infrastructure context. Metadata 'Revision Author' is closer to implementation context than canon identity, so acceptable, but if NTS prefers full canon-purity, could be normalized to 'Lane_03' only.
